apiKeys.title: API keys
apiKeys.description: Manage personal admin API keys for the MCP server and other automation.
apiKeys.yourKeysDescription: Keys belong to you alone. Treat them like passwords: anyone with the key can act as you against the admin MCP endpoint.
apiKeys.noKeysHint: Create one to connect an MCP client.
apiKeys.revokeKeyDescriptionNamed: “{{name}}” will stop working immediately. This cannot be undone.
apiKeys.revokeKeyDescriptionUnnamed: The key will stop working immediately. This cannot be undone.
apiKeys.typeNameToConfirm: Type the key name to confirm
apiKeys.typeIdToConfirm: Type the key ID to confirm
apiKeys.revokeFailedRetry: Couldn't revoke that key. Try again.
apiKeys.createDescription: The plaintext key will be shown <0>once</0>. Copy it before closing: there is no recovery flow.
apiKeys.scopeDescription: Read-only keys can inspect CMS content and assets. Read-write keys can create, update, publish, move, and delete.
apiKeys.createFailedRetry: Couldn't create that key. Try again.
apiKeys.expiryPositive: Expiry must be a positive number of days
apiKeys.revealDescription: This is the only time the plaintext key is shown. Closing this dialog hides it permanently.
apiKeys.mcpClientLabel: Add to your MCP client (Claude Desktop, etc.)

assets.title: Assets
assets.description: Upload, organize, and manage media. Drag files anywhere to upload.
assets.noAssetsHint: Drag files anywhere or click Upload to add some.
assets.moveDescription: Use a path like <0>/images/blog/</0> (must start and end with <1>/</1>). Leave blank to move to the root.
assets.deleteSelectedDescription: This permanently deletes the underlying files from storage. This action cannot be undone.
assets.singleDeleteDescriptionNamed: This permanently deletes {{mimeType}} from storage. This action cannot be undone.
assets.typeAssetIdToConfirm: Type the asset ID to confirm
assets.singleDeleteFailed: Couldn't delete that asset. Try again.

card.publicTitle: Cached on the shared CDN for 24h
card.restrictedTitle: Browser-cache only; never served from the CDN

cleanupPage.title: Asset cleanup
cleanupPage.description: Sweep orphan PENDING upload rows, unreferenced S3 objects under the <0>assets/</0> prefix, and incomplete multipart uploads. The same job runs automatically every 24 hours on the worker; this page is for on-demand inspection and execution.
cleanupPage.subDescription: Requires the BullMQ worker (<0>apps/worker</0>) to be running. The dry run below is a read-only DB + S3 scan and works without it; the <1>Run cleanup</1> button enqueues a job that only the worker can execute.
cleanupPage.dryRunDescription: Counts and a sample of what the cleanup job would touch. Nothing is deleted on this page until you click <0>Run cleanup</0>.
cleanupPage.orphanPendingDescription: Asset rows whose client stopped heartbeating more than 5 minutes ago. The underlying S3 object (if any) is also deleted.
cleanupPage.unrefObjectsDescription: Objects under assets/ that have no matching Asset row, older than 15 minutes (so live uploads aren't reaped).
cleanupPage.incompleteMultipartDescription: Multipart uploads initiated more than 24 hours ago and never completed. Quietly accrue storage costs until aborted.
cleanupPage.runDescription: Enqueues a job on the BullMQ <0>cleanup-assets</0> queue. The worker re-checks every candidate against fresh database state at delete time, so this is safe to run even with active uploads in flight.
cleanupPage.enqueued: Cleanup job enqueued — the worker will pick it up shortly.

devices.title: Devices
devices.description: Device codes created via the OAuth device-authorization flow. Denying a code stops future polling for that code; it does not sign out an already-issued session.
devices.recentDescription: Pending entries are awaiting your approval. Approved entries can be marked denied here to prevent further use of the code.
devices.noCodesHint: When an MCP client kicks off a device-authorization flow, the request shows up here.
devices.denyDescriptionNamed: Code {{code}} will be marked denied. The associated session, if any, is not signed out automatically.
devices.denyDescriptionUnnamed: The code will be marked denied.
devices.typeUserCodeToConfirm: Type the user code to confirm
devices.denyFailed: Couldn't deny that code. Try again.

localeTabs.translationPending: Translation pending — refresh in a few seconds.
localeTabs.retranslateDisabledTooltip: Manual edits will be lost — duplicate the entry first or revert via the translate worker.
localeTabs.retranslateConflict: This translation has manual edits — discard or copy them before re-translating.

markdown.assetEmbedHint: Embed assets with <0>![alt](asset:ID)</0>. Markdown supports headings, emphasis, lists, links, and code.

jobs.title: Jobs
jobs.description: Recent BullMQ failures across every production queue.
jobs.noneFailedDescription: The queues have no retained failed jobs. This page refreshes automatically while open.

seedPage.title: Run database seed
seedPage.description: Runs <0>runSeed()</0> from <0>packages/db/prisma/seed.ts</0> — the same function <0>prisma db seed</0> calls. This is a mechanism, not content: it does whatever your seed script does.
seedPage.workerNote: Requires the BullMQ worker (<0>apps/worker</0>) to be running — the run is enqueued as a job.
seedPage.emptyStubNote: The shipped seed script is an empty stub, so this does nothing until you write seed logic. Whatever you add must be idempotent — this button can be clicked repeatedly.
seedPage.prodWarningBody: This server is running in production. Seeding writes directly to the live database and cannot be undone. A stricter confirmation phrase is required.
seedPage.confirmDescription: This executes your seed script against the current database. Make sure it is idempotent before continuing.
seedPage.confirmDescriptionProd: PRODUCTION: this executes your seed script against the live database and cannot be undone. Only continue if you are certain the seed is idempotent and safe to run here.
seedPage.typePrompt: Type the phrase below to confirm
seedPage.enqueued: Seed job enqueued — the worker will pick it up shortly.
seedPage.enqueueFailed: Failed to start the seed job.
seedPage.jobEmpty: The seed script is empty — nothing was seeded. Add logic to packages/db/prisma/seed.ts.

overview.title: Admin overview
overview.description: Back office dashboard for provisioning, operations, access, media, and enterprise controls.

setupBanner.title: No administrators configured
setupBanner.message: Set <0>ADMIN_EMAILS</0> in your environment and restart the server to enable admin features. See your deployment's production hardening docs for details.
setupBanner.errorMessage: The app could not check whether administrators are configured. Review the server logs before publishing.

settings.title: Admin Settings
settings.deploymentDescription: Runtime profile and entitlement posture for this server.
settings.force2faInternalDescription: When enabled, admin users must set up two-factor authentication before they can use internal tools.
settings.force2faPublicDescription: When enabled, signed-in non-admin users must set up two-factor authentication before they can use the application.
settings.force2faAdminWarning: You must enable 2FA for your own account before you can require it for others. Go to Security settings to set it up.
settings.force2faInternalEnabled: Two-factor authentication is now required for administrators
settings.force2faInternalDisabled: Two-factor authentication is no longer required for administrators
settings.force2faPublicEnabled: Two-factor authentication is now required for public users
settings.force2faPublicDisabled: Two-factor authentication is no longer required for public users
settings.updateFailed: Couldn't update that setting. Try again.

users.title: Users
users.description: Invite new members, change roles, archive, or permanently delete accounts.
users.roleUpdateFailed: Couldn't update that role. Try again.
users.archiveFailed: Couldn't archive that user. Try again.
users.restoreFailed: Couldn't restore that user. Try again.
users.deleteFailed: Couldn't delete that user. Try again.
users.inviteDescription: Creates an account with a temporary password. The new user receives an email with their sign-in details (or you can copy them after creation if email isn't configured).
users.revealEmailedDescription: {{email}} has been emailed a temporary password. You can also copy it below if needed.
users.revealNotEmailedDescription: The account for {{email}} was created, but the email could not be sent. Share the temporary password with them through another channel.
users.tempPasswordHint: This password is only shown once. The user should change it after their first sign-in.
users.deleteDescription: This permanently removes the account, sessions, API keys, and devices. This cannot be undone. To preserve a user's authored content, archive them instead.
users.typeEmailToConfirm: Type the user's email to confirm

enterprise.title: Enterprise Logs
enterprise.description: Org-wide log collection policy and fine-tuning export artifacts.
enterprise.bootstrapDescription: Create the deployment org and make yourself the first org admin.
enterprise.exportsDescription: Generate deterministic artifacts for fine-tuning pipelines.
